statement: The system shall provide a baseline configuration and support variant configurations for extended arms, increased lift capacity, and waterproofing as required by a project.
The system shall enter safe mode when a movement not programmed to be understood is attempted or when a movement exceeds the system’s capability.
The system shall display the position within the range for each joint on the MMI screen and change the display colour when the joint approaches its limits.
The system shall allow a 50% extension beyond the original timeframe for any task that has a defined timeframe.
The system shall provide stops or guides to ensure the cable remains within the defined area.
The system shall model the whole scene exactly in simulation.
The system shall support teach files that allow programming a point and automatically drive the robot to that point.
The system shall allow programming of repeatable tasks and provide a home button that moves the robot to a known state.
The system shall move the robot close to the target autonomously and then hand over control to the operator.
The system shall provide subtle tactile feedback so the operator can feel when something is going incorrectly.
The system shall provide a variable viewpoint camera that can be moved around to improve depth perception.
The system shall provide fixed camera views that remain constant for specific tasks.
The system shall allow operators to zoom out and have the camera automatically follow their view as they move.
The system shall support optional 3D cameras.
The system shall prioritize image resolution over frame rate for precision tasks.
The system shall provide force feedback to indicate when to stop moving forward.
The system shall display a ghost mode that shows the predicted end position before the robot moves.
The system shall have no-go zones that prevent movement into hazardous areas.
The system shall sound an audio alarm when the robot approaches a no-go zone.
The system shall use computer vision to recognize materials such as aluminium or painted surfaces, enforce no‑go zones based on that information, and provide visual cues.
The system shall detect overload that causes the gripper fingers to open and provide feedback to prevent dropping objects.
